architecture.name: MedSecure Healthcare SaaS Platform v2
architecture.description: Enterprise healthcare SaaS platform serving 200+ hospitals, 3,000+ clinics, and 15 payers. Multi-tenant AKS deployment with FHIR R4 interoperability, PHI-hardened data tier, EHR integrations (Epic, Cerner, MEDITECH), telehealth, AI-assisted diagnostics, and clinical decision support. All PHI encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) and in transit (TLS 1.3 with mutual auth). HIPAA BAA with Azure. Zero-trust architecture with microsegmentation.

components.name: Angular 17 SPA — Patient Portal
components.technology: Angular 17.2, TypeScript 5.4, RxJS 7.8, Angular Material 17.2, SMART on FHIR client
components.notes: Patient-facing portal for scheduling, lab results, billing, messaging, telehealth. CSP enforced. PKCE OAuth2 flow. No PHI in localStorage/sessionStorage. Audit trail for all PHI access.

components.name: React 18 SPA — Clinician Dashboard
components.technology: React 18.3, TypeScript 5.4, TanStack Query 5.0, Zustand 4.5, SMART on FHIR Launch
components.notes: Provider-facing dashboard for patient records, clinical notes, order entry (CPOE), e-prescribing. Context-aware PHI redaction for non-clinical staff.

components.name: Azure Front Door Premium
components.technology: Azure Front Door Premium (DRS 2.1 + Bot Manager + Private Link)
components.notes: Global L7 load balancer with integrated WAF, DDoS protection (Azure DDoS Protection Standard), geo-filtering for ITAR compliance. No PHI cached at edge.

components.name: Azure Application Gateway v2 + WAF
components.technology: Application Gateway v2 (WAF_v2 SKU), Zone-redundant, OWASP 3.2 ruleset
components.notes: L7 ingress with SSL policy (TLS 1.2+), URL routing to AKS ingress, mutual TLS for EHR integrations, custom HIPAA-specific WAF rules blocking PHI in query strings.

components.name: AKS Cluster — Primary
components.technology: AKS 1.29.2, containerd 1.7, Azure CNI Overlay, Calico 3.27, KEDA 2.13, Dapr 1.13
components.notes: 3 system nodes (D4ds_v5) + 12 user nodes (D8ds_v5) + 4 GPU nodes (NC4as_T4_v3 for AI). Azure RBAC-integrated. Pod Security Standards: restricted. Ephemeral OS disks. Confidential computing nodes for PHI processing.

components.name: Azure API Management (APIM)
components.technology: APIM Premium tier, internal VNet mode, multi-region
components.notes: Central API gateway with rate limiting (1000 req/min per tenant), SMART on FHIR token validation, PHI audit logging, request/response transformation, API versioning.

components.name: FHIR R4 API Service
components.technology: ASP.NET Core 8.0, HAPI FHIR R4 server, HL7.Fhir.R4 SDK 5.7
components.notes: Full FHIR R4 REST API (Patient, Observation, Encounter, DiagnosticReport, MedicationRequest, Condition, Procedure, AllergyIntolerance). Supports SMART on FHIR launch, Bulk FHIR export ($export), Subscription notifications.

components.name: Patient Demographics Service
components.technology: Java 21 (Spring Boot 3.2.3, Spring Security 6.2), Flyway 10.7
components.notes: Master Patient Index (MPI) with probabilistic matching (Jaro-Winkler + ML). Patient merge/unmerge. Identity verification. Consent management per HIPAA Right of Access.

components.name: Clinical Workflow Engine
components.technology: Go 1.22, Temporal 1.23 (workflow orchestrator), gRPC
components.notes: Orchestrates clinical workflows: admissions (ADT), order processing (CPOE), referrals, care plans. Temporal-based saga patterns for multi-step clinical transactions. Compensation on failure.

components.name: Lab Results Service
components.technology: Python 3.12 (FastAPI 0.109, HL7 apy 0.4), HL7v2 ADT/ORU message parser
components.notes: Receives lab results via HL7v2 ORU messages and FHIR DiagnosticReport. Abnormal result alerting. Critical value notification (CAP requirement). Interface to Quest Diagnostics and LabCorp.

components.name: e-Prescribing Service (EPCS)
components.technology: Java 21 (Spring Boot 3.2.3), Surescripts NCPDP SCRIPT 2017071
components.notes: Electronic prescribing including controlled substances (EPCS per DEA 21 CFR 1311). Two-factor auth for Schedule II-V drugs. Drug-drug interaction checking via FDB MedKnowledge. Formulary checking.

components.name: Telehealth Service
components.technology: Node.js 20 LTS, WebRTC (mediasoup SFU 3.13), Socket.IO 4.7
components.notes: HIPAA-compliant video visits with end-to-end encryption (SRTP + DTLS). Screen sharing for imaging review. Virtual waiting room. Recording with signed consent. Integration with scheduling service.

components.name: Medical Imaging Service (DICOM)
components.technology: Python 3.12 (pydicom 2.4, Orthanc DICOM server 1.12, DICOMweb)
components.notes: PACS integration via DICOM C-STORE/C-FIND/C-MOVE. DICOMweb WADO-RS/STOW-RS. De-identification for research (DICOM Supplement 142). Zero-footprint DICOM viewer.

components.name: Clinical Decision Support (CDS)
components.technology: Python 3.12 (scikit-learn 1.4, PyTorch 2.2, CQL Engine), CDS Hooks 2.0
components.notes: AI-assisted CDS with CDS Hooks integration for EHRs. Sepsis early warning (SOFA score ML model). Drug interaction alerts. Clinical pathway recommendations. FDA 510(k) cleared for specific use cases.

components.name: Billing & Claims Service
components.technology: Java 21 (Spring Boot 3.2.3), X12 837/835 EDI, FHIR ExplanationOfBenefit
components.notes: Claims submission (X12 837P/837I), remittance (X12 835), eligibility (X12 270/271). ICD-10-CM/CPT coding assistance. Revenue cycle management. Clearinghouse integration (Change Healthcare, Availity).

components.name: Consent Management Service
components.technology: Go 1.22, FHIR Consent resource, blockchain-anchored audit trail
components.notes: Manages patient consent directives (HIPAA 164.508). Granular consent: per-provider, per-purpose, time-limited. Behavioral health segmentation (42 CFR Part 2). Consent enforcement at API gateway level.

components.name: Notification Service
components.technology: Node.js 20 LTS, Azure Communication Services, Twilio
components.notes: HIPAA-compliant notifications: SMS (opt-in, no PHI in text), email (encrypted), push (badge only). Appointment reminders, lab result alerts, prescription ready notifications. Patient communication preferences stored per consent.

components.name: Scheduling Service
components.technology: Java 21 (Spring Boot 3.2.3), FHIR Schedule/Slot
components.notes: Provider scheduling, patient self-scheduling, resource allocation (rooms, equipment). Waitlist management. Template-based recurring schedules. Integration with Epic and Cerner scheduling.

components.name: Azure Cosmos DB — Patient Records
components.technology: Cosmos DB (Core SQL API), multi-region writes, RU/s autoscale 400-40000
components.notes: FHIR resources (Patient, Observation, Condition, etc). Partition key: tenant_id/patient_id. Always-encrypted for PHI columns. Point-in-time restore (30 days). CMK via Key Vault. Analytical store for population health.

components.name: Azure SQL Database — Operational & Billing
components.technology: Azure SQL Database Business Critical tier, Always Encrypted with enclaves, TDE
components.notes: Claims, scheduling, billing, provider directory. Always Encrypted for SSN, DOB, MRN columns. Row-level security for multi-tenancy. Temporal tables for audit trail. Geo-replicated to DR region.

components.name: Azure Blob Storage — PHI Documents & DICOM
components.technology: Azure Blob Storage (Hot + Archive tiers), immutable storage with WORM compliance
components.notes: Clinical documents (CDA R2), scanned records, DICOM images, consent forms. SSE-CMK encryption. Immutable retention for compliance (7 years per HIPAA). Lifecycle management: hot→cool→archive. Private endpoint only.

components.name: Azure Event Hub — Clinical Event Stream
components.technology: Event Hub Premium (16 PUs), Kafka-compatible, Schema Registry
components.notes: Real-time clinical events: ADT messages, vital signs streams, lab results, medication administrations. Kafka protocol for cross-platform consumers. 7-day retention. Schema Registry enforces FHIR resource validation.

components.name: Azure Service Bus — Command Queue
components.technology: Azure Service Bus Premium (8 MU), sessions, duplicate detection, dead-letter
components.notes: Command/request patterns: order processing, claims submission, referral routing, prescription fulfillment. Message encryption in transit+rest. Session-based FIFO for clinical ordering.

components.name: Azure Logic Apps — FHIR/HL7 Integration Hub
components.technology: Logic Apps Standard (ISE), FHIR connector, HL7v2 connector, X12 connector
components.notes: Orchestrates EHR integrations: HL7v2 ADT/ORU/ORM message transformation, FHIR Bundle operations, X12 EDI translation. Retry with exponential backoff. Error handling with dead-letter queue.

components.name: Azure Key Vault HSM
components.technology: Azure Key Vault (Managed HSM pool, FIPS 140-2 Level 3)
components.notes: PHI encryption keys (CMK for Cosmos DB, SQL, Blob), TLS certificates, API signing keys, EPCS digital signing certificates. Soft-delete + purge protection. Access policies: minimum privilege.

components.name: Azure AD B2C — Patient Identity
components.technology: Azure AD B2C, Custom policies (Identity Experience Framework), MFA
components.notes: Patient authentication: email+password, social (Google, Apple), phone OTP. SMART on FHIR launch with PKCE. Progressive profiling. Identity proofing for prescription access. Risk-based conditional access.

components.name: Azure AD — Staff Identity (Entra ID)
components.technology: Entra ID P2, Conditional Access, PIM, FIDO2 passwordless
components.notes: Clinician/admin authentication with MFA enforced. Break-the-glass access for emergency situations (logged + alerted). FIDO2 security keys for EPCS two-factor. Privileged Identity Management for admin roles.

components.name: Azure Cache for Redis
components.technology: Azure Cache for Redis Enterprise (E10, cluster mode), TLS-only
components.notes: Session state, API response cache (non-PHI only), feature flags, rate limit counters. No PHI stored in cache (enforced by application layer). AOF persistence for session durability.

components.name: Azure Container Registry
components.technology: ACR Premium, content trust (Docker Notary v2), vulnerability scanning (Qualys)
components.notes: Private container registry. Image signing required for production. Quarantine policy for vulnerable images. Geo-replicated to DR region. Integrated with AKS via managed identity.

components.name: Azure Monitor + Application Insights
components.technology: Azure Monitor, App Insights 3.0, Log Analytics (KQL), Azure Managed Grafana
components.notes: APM, distributed tracing, custom metrics, alerting. PHI scrubbing in logs (PII redaction pipeline). Custom dashboards for SLA monitoring. Alert rules: p95 latency, error rate, PHI access anomalies.

components.name: Azure Defender for Cloud
components.technology: Microsoft Defender for Cloud (CSPM + CWP), Defender for Containers, Defender for SQL
components.notes: Continuous security assessment. HIPAA regulatory compliance dashboard. Just-in-time VM access. Adaptive network hardening. File integrity monitoring. Threat detection for SQL injection, anomalous database queries.

components.name: Microsoft Sentinel — SIEM/SOAR
components.technology: Microsoft Sentinel, UEBA, Fusion ML, custom analytics rules, playbooks (Logic Apps)
components.notes: HIPAA-specific detection rules: unauthorized PHI access, bulk record export, after-hours clinical access, VIP patient snooping. SOAR playbooks for automated response. Integration with ServiceNow for ticketing.

components.name: Epic EHR Integration (Interconnect)
components.technology: Epic Interconnect, SMART on FHIR R4, HL7v2 MLLP, Epic MyChart APIs
components.notes: Bidirectional integration with Epic: patient demographics (ADT), orders (ORM), results (ORU), clinical documents (CDA). SMART on FHIR app launch from Epic. MyChart integration for patient portal SSO.

components.name: Cerner (Oracle Health) Integration
components.technology: Oracle Health FHIR R4, Cerner Millennium HL7v2, Open API
components.notes: Integration with Cerner Millennium: patient lookup, order entry, result retrieval. FHIR R4 preferred, HL7v2 fallback for legacy Millennium instances.

components.name: MEDITECH Expanse Integration
components.technology: MEDITECH Expanse FHIR R4, HL7v2, MIS Traverse
components.notes: Integration with MEDITECH Expanse for community hospitals. Focus on ADT, lab orders/results, and pharmacy. MIS Traverse for real-time data access.

components.name: Surescripts e-Prescribing Gateway
components.technology: Surescripts Network, NCPDP SCRIPT 2017071, real-time prescription benefit (RTPB)
components.notes: Electronic prescribing network: NewRx, RxRenewal, RxFill, CancelRx. Medication history retrieval. Real-time formulary and benefit checking. EPCS-certified.

components.name: Clearinghouse Integration (Change Healthcare)
components.technology: Change Healthcare API, X12 EDI (270/271, 276/277, 837/835), SFTP fallback
components.notes: Claims clearinghouse for payer connectivity. Real-time eligibility, claim status, ERA/EFT. Breach monitoring following 2024 Change Healthcare incident — added Availity as backup.

components.name: Azure Private DNS + VNet
components.technology: Azure VNet (10.0.0.0/8), Private DNS Zones, NSG, Azure Firewall Premium
components.notes: Hub-spoke VNet topology. Subnets: AKS (10.1.0.0/16), Data (10.2.0.0/16), Integration (10.3.0.0/16), Management (10.4.0.0/16). All PaaS via Private Endpoints. Azure Firewall with IDPS for east-west traffic inspection.

components.name: Azure Policy + Blueprint (HIPAA)
components.technology: Azure Policy (HIPAA HITRUST 9.2 initiative), Azure Blueprints, custom deny policies
components.notes: Enforced policies: encryption-at-rest required, TLS 1.2 minimum, diagnostic logging enabled, no public endpoints for data services, CMK required for PHI stores, network access restricted to VNet.

components.name: Genomics Analysis Service
components.technology: Python 3.12 (htslib 1.19, pysam 0.22, VEP API), Azure Batch for compute
components.notes: Pharmacogenomics (PGx) analysis for personalized medicine. VCF file processing. Variant annotation (ClinVar, gnomAD). GINA Act compliance for genetic non-discrimination. Data segregated from standard PHI per additional consent.

components.name: Population Health Analytics
components.technology: Azure Synapse Analytics, Power BI Embedded, Apache Spark 3.5
components.notes: Cohort analysis, disease prevalence, outcomes tracking, quality measures (HEDIS, MIPS). De-identification pipeline (Safe Harbor + Expert Determination per HIPAA 164.514). Dedicated Synapse workspace with PHI-scoped access.

components.name: Patient Matching & MPI Service
components.technology: Java 21, probabilistic matching (Jaro-Winkler + ML ensemble), Verato MPI integration
components.notes: Enterprise MPI with 99.2% match accuracy. Duplicate detection, patient merge/unmerge workflows. Referential matching via Verato Universal MPI. Cross-organization patient linking for HIE participation.

components.name: Health Information Exchange (HIE) Gateway
components.technology: Carequality/CommonWell frameworks, IHE XDS.b/XCA, TEFCA QHIN gateway
components.notes: Trusted Exchange Framework and Common Agreement (TEFCA) participant. Document query/retrieve via IHE profiles. Consent-based access control. Patient record locator service.

components.name: Azure Managed Identity + Workload Identity
components.technology: Azure Managed Identity, Workload Identity Federation, SPIFFE/SPIRE
components.notes: Zero-trust service-to-service authentication. No stored credentials. Pod-level identity binding via SPIFFE. Automatic certificate rotation. Used by all microservices to access Azure PaaS.

components.name: Audit & Compliance Service
components.technology: Go 1.22, append-only audit log, FHIR AuditEvent resource, ATNA (IHE)
components.notes: Immutable audit trail for all PHI access (HIPAA 164.312(b)). FHIR AuditEvent for each operation. IHE ATNA audit messages to SIEM. Who accessed what patient data, when, from where, and why (break-the-glass justification).

components.name: Temporal Workflow Server
components.technology: Temporal Server 1.23 (Cassandra backend), TLS mTLS, namespace isolation
components.notes: Workflow orchestration for long-running clinical processes: multi-step order fulfillment, claims adjudication, referral tracking. Namespace per tenant. mTLS between workers and server.

components.name: Azure Backup & DR
components.technology: Azure Backup, Azure Site Recovery, Cross-region replication
components.notes: RPO: 1 hour, RTO: 4 hours. Geo-redundant backup vault (RA-GRS). Automated DR drills quarterly. Cosmos DB continuous backup with PITR. SQL geo-replication to DR region with automatic failover group.

components.name: Azure Confidential Computing (ACC) Enclave
components.technology: Azure Confidential VMs (DCsv3), Intel SGX enclaves, Open Enclave SDK
components.notes: Confidential computing for most sensitive PHI processing: genomics analysis, behavioral health records (42 CFR Part 2), substance abuse records. Data encrypted during processing (not just at rest/transit).

components.name: Research Data Platform (De-identified)
components.technology: Azure Databricks, Delta Lake 3.1, MLflow 2.10, differential privacy (OpenDP)
components.notes: De-identified datasets for clinical research. HIPAA Safe Harbor method + k-anonymity (k=5). Differential privacy for statistical queries. IRB-approved research projects only. Separate Azure subscription.

components.name: API Rate Limiter & DDoS Shield
components.technology: Azure DDoS Protection Standard, custom rate limiter (Redis-backed), circuit breaker
components.notes: Per-tenant rate limiting: 1000 req/min standard, 5000 req/min premium. Circuit breaker per downstream service. DDoS: volumetric, protocol, and L7 application attacks. Geo-blocking for sanctioned countries.

components.name: Certificate Manager (TLS/mTLS)
components.technology: Azure Key Vault + cert-manager (Kubernetes), DigiCert CA for external, internal CA for mTLS
components.notes: Automated certificate lifecycle. External: DigiCert EV for patient/clinician portals. Internal: Private CA for service mesh mTLS. EPCS: dedicated signing certificates per DEA requirements. Auto-renewal 30 days before expiry.

components.name: Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Engine
components.technology: Microsoft Purview DLP, custom PHI regex patterns, ML-based PHI detection
components.notes: Prevents PHI leakage in: API responses to unauthorized consumers, log files, error messages, email/notifications. Custom patterns for MRN, SSN, DOB, diagnosis codes. Blocks PHI in browser console (CSP violation reporting).

connections.notes: Patient service → SQL with Always Encrypted
Lab results → Logic Apps for HL7 transform

trust_boundaries.name: Internet → Edge (Untrusted → DMZ)
trust_boundaries.description: Public internet traffic enters through Azure Front Door Premium with DDoS protection and WAF. All PHI-bearing requests must be authenticated before crossing this boundary.

trust_boundaries.name: Edge → Application (DMZ → VNet)
trust_boundaries.description: Authenticated traffic flows from edge tier into the application VNet via APIM. All requests validated for proper OAuth2 tokens, SMART scopes, and tenant context.

trust_boundaries.name: Application → Data (VNet → Private Endpoints)
trust_boundaries.description: Application services access data stores exclusively via Azure Private Endpoints. No public endpoints on any data service. All PHI encrypted with CMK.

trust_boundaries.name: Application → External EHR (VNet → Partner Networks)
trust_boundaries.description: Integration with external EHR systems (Epic, Cerner, MEDITECH) crosses organizational trust boundaries. All connections via dedicated VPN tunnels or ExpressRoute with mTLS.

trust_boundaries.name: Application → Research (PHI → De-identified)
trust_boundaries.description: Strict de-identification boundary. PHI data must be transformed via Safe Harbor method (18 identifiers removed) before crossing into research analytics.

trust_boundaries.name: Standard → Confidential (VNet → SGX Enclaves)
trust_boundaries.description: Most sensitive data (genomics, behavioral health, substance abuse) processed in hardware-isolated confidential computing enclaves. Data encrypted even during processing.

trust_boundaries.name: HIE/TEFCA Exchange Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Health Information Exchange boundary per TEFCA framework. Patient consent must be verified before any cross-organization data sharing.
